observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make representation to the PhilHealth that the Municipality will follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received by the Municipal Government from PhilHealth for services rendered to patients with PhilHealth coverage.

We recommended the management to direct the MPDC in coordination of the GFPS to complete the establishment of GAD database containing SDD to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.
We recommended the GFPS to conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended the MGSO to:

Strictly comply Section 3.2 of COA Circular No.2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 by submitting the POs and the required supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) working days after its perfection; and

Notify the Audit Team and/or submit request for inspection of goods within 48 hours after delivery to conduct inspection of the delivered goods and validate completeness and compliance with specifications.

The Municipal Mayor to direct the following concerned officials:

The Municipal Engineer to submit to the MA Certificates of Project Completion/Turn-over to facilitate transfer of completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts;

The MA to reclassify the completed projects from CIP to appropriate PPE accounts and provide the corresponding depreciation in the books, pursuant to the provision of NGAS Manual; and

The Municipal Treasurer to return the unexpended balance of fund transfers to its source agency or Bureau of Treasury, as the case may be, to fully liquidate the funds received.

The Municipal Mayor and the SB to look into the possibility of creating an Ordinance for the establishment of Special Accounts so that revenues, expenditures and results of operations of an enterprise can be readily assessed and validated.

We recommended, and the management agreed to the following:
The MGSO to:
Develop and maintain a comprehensive Road Map to accurately determine all road lots owned by the Municipality and submit the same to the Municipal Engineer; and
Submit a report on Local Road Network at the end of the accounting period using the prescribed format in Annex A of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
The Municipal Engineer to:
Assist the Municipal Assessor in surveying/measuring the undeclared road lots of the Municipality based on the updated Road Map; and
Prepare and maintain a Report on Road Networks of the LGU to include road lots with complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects of the Municipality as of reporting period, and provide the same to the MA and MGSO.
The Municipal Assessor to:
Use the updated Road Map and detailed report of the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO), determine the acquisition costs of the undeclared road lots duly supported by a tax declaration in the name of the LGU; and
Submit an official valuation report of all road lots, using the acquisition costs upon purchase/donation, to the offices of the MA, MGSO and Municipal Engineer for recording in the books.
The MA to:
Record the acquisition costs of the road lots through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher based on the valuation report submitted by the Municipal Assessor;
Maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) to track and monitor road lot valuations; and
Provide proper disclosure of the road network system in the Notes to the FS.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Engineer:
Immediately submit to the MAO the SWA, Certificates of Actual Inspection, Completion, Acceptance and Turnover and other relevant documents for all completed projects to the Office of the MA for proper accounting thereof, particularly the reclassification of the completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts; and
Prepare the QRPGPPA and submit a copy of the same to the audit team at the end of every quarter to facilitate proper recording and monitoring of projects/programs/activities.
The MA, upon receipt of the documents from the MEO, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reclassification of the completed projects from CIP to appropriate PPE accounts and recognition of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation for the completed projects.
The MA and Municipal Engineer perform reconciliation and periodic monitoring of their records to ensure proper recording and monitoring of the CIP projects.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Engineer immediately submits to the MGSO a written report on the three projects specifying the necessary details as to its demolition/damage as well as other documents to prove the same;
Upon receipt of the written report from the Municipal Engineer, the MGSO records the same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and submits a copy to the MA; and
The MA, upon receipt of the IIRUP, prepares the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the projects from the books of accounts.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The MGSO promptly prepares the monthly S/RSMI duly supported by approved RIS and its supporting documents such as Distribution List/Sheets and ICS, and timely submit the same to the MA so that issuances thereof are timely recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts;
The MAO monitors the inventory balances and coordinate with the MGSO for the submission of the S/RSMI and its supporting documents, and draw a JEV to adjust the inventory accounts and its related expense account; and
The MAO and the MGSO conduct regular reconciliation of their records to ensure that inventories and issuances recorded in the books are reconciled with the MGSO’s records as of December 31, 2024.
(For Fuel)
We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) establish control mechanisms to ensure the proper coordination of its Office with the MGSO on the maintenance of records for fuel consumption; and
The MGSO substantiate the accuracy of the quantity and amount appearing in the FCR and inventory report stored in the fuel storage tank through the submission/presentation of additional documents/records, such as RIV, Daily Gas Issue Record, Summary of Daily Totals of RIV, Gasoline/Fuel Control Cards to ensure proper monitoring and reporting in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The HRMO, during budget preparations of the Annual Budget for the year, include not only the retirable/co-terminous employees and elected officials during the Annual Budget period but also those employees/officials who will retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the computation of the terminal leave benefits;
The Local Finance Committee (LFC) prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO (in letter a above) in the Annual Budget for the current year;
The MA to:
Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits that are expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period, supported by appropriation and cash backup duly certified by the MBO and Municipal Treasurer. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Prior Period Adjustments			₱4.501 million
Leave Benefits Payable			₱4.501 million
To recognize prior years’ accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase of salary in CY 2025;
Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required disclosures in the Notes to FS, specifically on the aggregate renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
Provide disclosure in the Notes to FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The MA to draw a JEV:
To adjust the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts; and
For the establishment of RPT/SET Receivables and its Deferred RPT/SET account balances only after receiving the MTO’s certified list of taxpayers.
The MAO and MTO to conduct a reconciliation of their records to ensure that the reported RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts in the books align with the MTO’s Summary of Tax Delinquencies as of year-end; and
The MTO to prepare the certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, based on the RPTARs it maintains.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The MA properly review the transactions and ensure that all financial transactions are properly classified and recorded in accordance with the RCALGU as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure that expenses are recognized under the appropriate accounts.
The MBO shall ensure that budget appropriations are correctly aligned to their corresponding expense accounts, in accordance with prescribed accounting classifications. Proper alignment between budgeting and accounting will prevent misclassification of expenses.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The MGSO:
Prepare the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs for items described as “non-existing” or “missing” in the RPCPPE following the format in Annex C of COA Circular 2020-006 and undertake the disposition and derecognition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs as provided in Sections 7 and 8 of the above Circular; and
Prepare the IIRUP for all PPEs found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed using the format prescribed in Annex 51 of Volume 2, NGAS for LGUS.
The MA, upon receipt of the List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs, take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of PPE and to set up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer/personnel, pursuant to Paragraphs 7.8 to 7.9 of the Circular.
The LCE constitute a Disposal Committee, and upon receipt of the IIRUP from MGSO commence disposal procedures in accordance with, COA-Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024, Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.

We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The MEO:
Implement a real-time tracking system to monitor the status of project implementation; and
Expedite the implementation of unimplemented PPAs to maximize the effective use of allocated funds.
The LDC through the MPDC ensure that PPAs are properly and specifically identified and costed at the planning stage to avoid delays in the execution of the PPAs; and
The LFC review the status of fund utilization of 20 per cent DF and report its progress to the LCE for immediate intervention and timely implementation of PPAs.

Moving forward, we recommended, and the management agreed to:
Exercise prudence in the disbursement of the LDRRMF and immediately stop the practice of charging expenses that are not directly aligned to disaster response and emergency operations;
Consider increasing the MOOE allocation under the GF for the MDRRMO to cover administrative and operational costs; and
Provide a separate allocation in the GF for insurance coverage of the LGU properties.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the GFPS in coordination with the MPDC:
Update the GAD database by conducting gender analysis to identify gender issues/gaps and determine gender-responsiveness PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB;
Ensure that proposed GAD PPAs of departments for allocation are supported by program profile containing the identified gender issues/GAD mandates and gender analysis situation of the community; and
Provide refresher training to the GFPS, and the latter technical assistance to the TWG, and department heads on gender analysis, GAD mainstreaming, and effective utilization of GAD data.

We recommended, and the LSB agreed to:
Ensure that all quarterly and annual reports are submitted to the DepEd CO and that copies be furnished to the SB, and the Regional Offices of the DBM IX and DILG IX not later than 20th day after the end of the quarter and for the annual report not later than the 15th day of February of the following year.
Designate a compliance officer within the LSB to track and verify submissions, ensuring that all required agencies receive copies on or before the given schedule.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the members of the MSWMB, headed by the LCE:
Formulate/craft specific mechanics and guidelines to implement the provisions of RA 9003 as provided in Municipal Ordinance No. 20-06 on segregation and collection of solid waste to include specific duties and responsibilities of households, businesses, barangays, and the municipal TWG in proper waste management.
Strictly implement waste segregation at the source: Businesses and households should be encouraged or required to segregate waste at source—separating biodegradable and non-biodegradable materials, by taking action as follows:
Conduct an education and information campaign in coordination with the barangays; and
Provide proper waste bins for segregation in a designated area.
Propose amendments to Municipal Ordinance No. 20-06 and include the following:
Establishment of MRFs in every barangay.
The specific types of waste to be collected at both barangay and municipal levels.
Clear enforcement mechanisms, including penalties for non-compliance and incentives for compliance.
Prioritize assigning JO personnel to handle the sorting and processing of waste to reduce the backlog and prevent further accumulation of unsorted waste.

We recommended, and the management agreed that through the LCE, consider allocating a budget for the purchase of a garbage truck with a compartment and/or devising the vehicle to ensure that it is fully covered to contain waste during collection and transportation.

We recommended:

The Municipal Treasurer to regularly maintain and update the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively, in order to come-up with a schedule of RPT and SET Receivables and facilitate reconciliation with accounting records; and 

The MA coordinate with MTO personnel in-charge in RPT and SET to reconcile both records and make adjustment, if necessary.

We recommended:

The Municipal Engineer to immediately submit to the MGSO the FCR of the FOL products upon final completion of the three FMR projects duly supported with daily Trip Tickets and Withdrawal Slips for inclusion in the SSMI;

The MGSO record the issuance in the Stock Cards and prepare/ submit to the MA the SSMI on FOL products to facilitate timely recording and adjustment of the FOL Inventory and the cost of FMR in the books, Upon receipt of the FCR and other liquidation documents; and 

The MA make necessary adjustment in the books particularly on FOL Inventory and Road Network accounts, upon receipt SSMI containing liquidation documents of the FOL products used in the NTF-ELCAC projects.

We recommended the MA to:

Draw a JEV to adjust the CIB-LCCA account No. 2552-1098-08 and 2552-1098-16 the amount ₱48,283.30 and ₱25,900, respectively and reclassify it as Due from GOCCs (Receivable);

Record the ACPN as Due from GOCCs and recognize only cash in bank upon actual transfer/credit to the LGU bank accounts, moving forward; and

Maintain subsidiary records of claims based on ACPN received and actual credits to LGUs bank accounts.

We recommended management to:

Direct the MBO to secure authority from the SB thru a Resolution before charging any expenditure against the Lumpsum Appropriation, moving forward.

Direct the MPDC to:

Specify the details of the currently identified PPAs charged against the 20% DF indicating the project location, specific implementation schedule and physical target;

Initiate the preparation of necessary documents to support the estimate of projects funded under the 20% DF before budget preparation; and

Develop and recommend other procedures to enhance the current practice regarding planning and budgeting of the 20% DF to avoid generic and lump-sum projects.

We recommended the management to:

Stop charging ineligible expenses to GAD Fund; and

Strictly adhere to the regulation in the utilization of GAD Fund in accordance with Annex 7 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-1.

We recommended the management to:

Direct the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB to endorse the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and

Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended the management to:

Post the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity concerned for seven calendar days as certified by the head of the BAC Secretariat of the Procuring Entity concerned per Section 21.1(b) of the RIRR, RA 9184;

Provide additional policies and sanctions to prevent nuisance/uninterested bidders that may undermine or even rigged the result of the procurement process; and

Strictly require the BAC-Technical Working Group to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the S/LCB in order to properly determine whether the S/LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA 9184.

We recommended the management the following:

The Municipal Mayor and SB shall be guided by the provisions of Section 322 and 336 of RA 7160 and ensure that savings from PS and MOOE are re-appropriated thru Supplemental Budget duly approved by the SB; and

The MBO and MA to properly advise the Municipal Mayor and SB on the use of savings other than augmentation and refrain from disbursing funds without proper authority from the LCE and SB, as the case maybe.
We recommended the Municipal Engineer to ensure that 100 per cent completion of projects are accurately reported in the SWA before submitting to the MA for payments.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the following officials:

The Municipal Engineer to:

Submit Assessment Report of the damaged portion of the projects, copy furnished the MA;

Conduct detailed engineering studies to ensure effectiveness of the project design and specifications for future similar projects; and

The MA to make necessary adjustments in the books to facilitate derecognition the equivalent cost of damaged portion of the CIP-Infra that has no future economic beneficial use to the agency Based on the Assessment Report of the Municipal Engineer.
The management to consider the amendment of the Municipal Revenue Code.

We recommended that Management: Require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and Municipal Accountant to observe the procedures outlined in Section 6 and 7 of the COA Circular 2020-006 to be able to come with a correct and reliable RPCPPE that reconciled with accounting records; Require the Municipal Engineer to review and evaluate the cost component of all on-going projects under the CIP to identify those that are actually completed to facilitate recording/transfer of its cost to the appropriate account classification; Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties turned-over to the barangays and other entities to facilitate proper derecognition and transfer of accounts; and Institute security measures to limit access of assets to authorized personnel only and maintain appropriate records as to whereabouts of equipment, vehicles and heavy equipment.

Require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to perpetually maintain Stock Cards for all inventory items and to record the receipts and issuance thereof in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual; and Promptly prepare and submit the same to the Accountant the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI), duly supported with RIS and FCR so that issuance thereof is timely recorded and accounted for in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LGU adopt a policy requiring the business owner to submit their income tax return to support the sworn statement of sales declaration in order to ensure that the declared sales is more or less reliable as a basis for assessment of business tax.   Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to utilize the Alpha List of Income Tax Payor prepared by the Municipal Accountant as reference in making business tax assessment. Lastly, we recommended that the management consider the amendment of the Municipal Revenue Code.

We revised our recommendations for the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Engineer to institute legal actions against the contractor for failure to undertake and/or complete the project within the periods specified in the contract, including but not limited to: Require the return of the collected Mobilization Fee of P1,335,322.47 (for Concreting of Moyo-Pase FMR); Prepare and submit Evaluation Report on the Contractor’s Accomplishment as validated by ARCP II on the Rehab with two km Concreting of Bolanao-Litolet FMR to determine the receivable or payable from the contractor; and Return to DAR-ARCP II any unused project funds and draw necessary journal entry to adjust/correct the Advances to Contractor, Due to NGAs & CIP accounts.

We reiterated that the: Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20 per cent Development Fund and their implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the development projects that could be implemented during the year by taking into consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality. Consider changes in the annual budget for projects which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

1.7 Recommendation:
1.7.1 Required the MGSO to perpetually maintain Stock Cards all inventory items and to record the
receipts and issuance thereof in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual.
1.7.2 Promptly prepare and submit the same to the MA the monthly RSMI, duly supported with
correct/valid RIS and FCR so that issuances thereof are timely recorded and accounted for in the
books of accounts.
1.7.3 Coordinate/Reconcile with the supplier’s records to come-up with an updated and correct
inventory balance of FOL products that will serve as basis in adjusting the accounting records.

2.4 Recommendation
2.4.1 We recommended the MM to require the personnel assigned to conduct inventory of medical
equipment to pursue the conduct of physical inventory and submit the RPCPPE as soon as
possible to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records and cleansing of PPE.

3.5 Recommendation
3.5.1 Required the MM
3.5.1.1 To direct the OIC-MT to intensify collection efforts in order to meet or surpass its revenue
targets by looking into the revenue objects that need to be prioritized for collection.
3.5.1.2 Consider providing incentives to collectors to encourage efficient performance.

4.7 Recommendation
4.7.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to implement fully the programs, projects and
activities under mitigation fund in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to equip the LGU
officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare and manage disaster. Particularly,
the MDRRMO to:
4.7.1.1 Request augmentation of funds to facilitate implementation of early warning system,
including procurement and installation of essential devices.
4.7.1.2 Conduct Disaster Risk Assessment to identify potential risk areas in the Municipality;
and
4.7.1.3 Constantly coordinate with Municipal Engineer and the BAC to facilitate procurement of
infrastructures, supplies, materials and equipment needed for disaster responses and
recovery operations on planned time frame.
4.7.2 In addition, the LCE to require the LDRRM Council to constantly monitor the full implementation
of the PPAs.

5.5 Recommendation
5.5.1 We reiterated that the:
5.5.1.1 Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the
provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the
programs/projects/activities funded by 20 per cent Development Fund and their
implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP were fully
implemented for the benefit of the constituents;
5.5.1.2 Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize the
development projects that could be implemented during the year by taking into
consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality. Consider changing the PPAs
in the AIPs which cannot be implemented during the year to other viable developmental
projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and
5.5.1.3 Consider the creation of a unit under the Office of the Municipal Mayor to purposely
monitor the various identified PPAs to be implemented during the year funded by the 20
per cent DF, LDRRMF and other funds coming from local and national agencies.

6.10 Recommendations
6.10.1 To strengthen the risk management capabilities of the Municipal Government and enable it to
manage its vulnerabilities in the event of disruptive events, we recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform
the following activities:
6.10.1.1 to conduct risk assessment;
6.10.1.2 to identify critical processes and functions;
6.10.1.3 to determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
6.10.1.4 to conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
6.10.1.5 to formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical processes and functions
and its related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the
determined disruptions following the prescribed template and be guided by the PSCP
guidebook; and
6.10.1.6 to submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the LCE for approval, and to the
Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.
6.10.2 We also recommended that Management improve their monitoring of operational requirements
like the submission of PSCP.

8.7.1 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to
establish GAD database containing sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information
for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive
PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

8.5 Recommendation
8.5.1 We recommended that the management to create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team
in consonance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to
evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

9.6 Recommendation
9.6.1 The MM to require the Municipal Agricultural Officer (MAO) to
9.6.1.1 Conduct inventory of all agricultural supplies on stock to determine the actual usable and
spoiled inventories;
9.6.1.2 Distribute the remaining usable seeds and fertilizers to the targeted beneficiaries to avoid
further damage and deterioration;
9.6.1.3 Dispose damaged/spoiled farm inputs thru dumping or other allowable method of
disposal to avoid environmental hazard and contamination; and
9.6.1.4 For future similar undertakings, the MAO should properly plan and execute the activities
to ensure efficient and effective implementation of the programs and achieved optimum
outcomes.

10.5 Recommendation
10.5.1 The management to send letter to the Department of Agriculture seeking from the latter the
Memorandum of Agreement to determine its responsibility on the corn-shellers.
10.5.2 The MM to require MAO to:
10.5.2.1 Cause the immediate distribution of corn shellers to the targeted beneficiaries to avoid
deterioration and damaged of the machines; and
10.5.2.2 Find ways and means to utilize abandoned facilities
10.5.3 For future similar projects, the MM, MPDC and other concerned officials shall properly plan
and select project locations/sites to ensure its sustainability taking into consideration the
environmental conditions such as source of water, population density and the likes.

11.3 Recommendation
11.3.1 Institute security measures to limit access of assets to authorized personnel only and require
the maintenance of appropriate records as to whereabouts of equipment, vehicles and heavy
equipment.

12.7 Recommendation
12.7.1 We required the MM and the Sangguniang Bayan to look into the possibility of creating an
Ordinance for the establishment of Special Accounts so that revenues, expenditures and
results of operations of an enterprise can be readily assessed and validated.

13.5 Recommendation
13.5.1 We recommended that the MM to direct the ME to notify and require the Contractor to rectify
the defects noted in the COA TAS Inspection Report to avoid further damage and early
deterioration of the road project.

14.5 Recommendation
14.5.1 We recommended that the MM to prioritize and consider the filling-up of the position of MGSO
or expedite promotion of deserving employees to properly handle and carry-out the functions,
duties and responsibilities thereof, with the views of providing/generating accurate and
reliable property and supply records.

24.1.4 Recommendation
20.1.4.1 We recommended the MA:
20.1.4.1.1 To make the necessary adjusting entries in the book of accounts in order
to reflect the correct Cash in Bank balance as shown in the Adjusted
Cash in Bank balance per Bank Reconciliation and to correctly state the
balances of the affected accounts whether real or nominal shown in the
FS as of December 31, 2021; and
20.1.4.1.2 To ensure that all valid reconciling items be recorded and adjusted in the
books are properly supported with complete documents,

25.1 We reiterated our recommendation that the management consider the amendment of the Municipal
Revenue Code.

26.1 We reiterated our recommendations that the management:
26.1.1 Prepare and submit Evaluation Report on the Contractor’s Accomplishment as validated by
ARCP II on the Rehab with two km Concreting of Bolanao-Litolet FMR to determine the
receivable or payable from the contractor; and
26.1.2 Return to DAR-ARCP II any unused project funds and draw necessary journal entry to adjust/correct
the Advances to Contractor, Due to NGAs & CIP accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management: Conduct proper disposal proceedings on unserviceable and beyond economic repair PPEs so that the LGU could generate income from the proceeds of the sale which can be used to augment its operational requirements and lessen the burden of keeping the accounting and property records for unserviceable items; and

We revised our recommendation that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to segregate the components of the Road Networks, namely: (a) road lot; (b) road pavement; (c) drainage and slope protection structures; and (d) other miscellaneous structures, as the case may be, guided by the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008, to accurately reflect the annual Depreciation Expense, Accumulated Depreciation, and the Net Book Value of the account. Moreover, provide depreciation for the aforementioned infrastructure assets in the ensuing year and adjust accordingly the corresponding accumulated depreciation in the government equity.

Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds; and Instruct the Accounting personnel assigned to journalize the financial transactions to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal account in the different funds affected and for the Municipal Accountant to constantly review the recording of such transactions.

Conduct intensive Information, Education and Communication (IEC) campaign on proper segregation of wastes at source through forums, focus group discussions and trainings/workshops to apprise the people on strict implementation of the Mandatory Segregation of Solid Wastes policy; Firmly enforce the existing Municipal Ordinance No. 15-24 in order to support the Mandatory Segregation of Solid Waste policy of RA 9003 to achieve minimization and reduction of wastes; and Distribute the remaining MRF to the target recipient barangays to facilitate segregation of garbage the barangay levels.

We reiterated our recommendation that management comply Section 37 of RA 9003 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing open dump site and convert it into an Eco-Park with Residual Containment Area (RCA) or sanitary landfill facility.
To acquire parcel of land for the construction of the motor pool.
Blacklist the contractor and disqualify them from participating in future procurement projects of the LGU;

Recommendation
1.5 We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
1.5.1 Conduct proper disposal proceedings on unserviceable and beyond

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the inventory committee  review  their  respective
reports  and  reconcile   with   the   accounting   unit   on   the   public
infrastructures that should be derecognized in the books of accounts of  the
LGU for having no future services potential. The  guidelines  set  forth  in
COA Circular  No.  2015-008  should  be  applied  to  for  the  recognition,
subsequent measurement and derecognition of assets to ensure  that  accounts
are properly valued and recorded.

       Moreover,  after  derecognition  of  the  inexistent  and  no   value
infrastructure assets have been made, we recommend the  adjustments  on  the
errors of depreciation  of  the  individual  accounts  discussed  above  and
enumerated in Appendix 10.

We recommended that Management set a  more  realistic  forecasting  of
revenue, taking into account prior  years’  experience  and  other  relevant
factors. Likewise, periodic review of the concerned  officials  should  also
be undertaken to ascertain whether collections targets are attainable.  This
will provide the assurance of improved finances for the  operations  of  the
Municipality.

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials closely  monitor
and  ensure  the  efficient  implementation  of  all  priority   development
projects and programs under  the  20%  development  fund  and  maximize  the
utilization of the said fund to help in the attainment of  social,  economic
and environmental development.

We recommended that Management facilitate the maintenance  of  special
account in the General Fund for the 20% development fund as  mandated  under
Section 313 of RA 7160.

      We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  consider  opening  a
separate bank account for the 20%  development  fund  since  this  fund  has
material balances and constant reconciliation of balances  per  actual  cash
backup and appropriation is necessary for the attainment of  the  objectives
of which the fund was created.

The Municipal Engineer shall closely  monitor  the  implementation  of
ADM  projects  to  ensure  completion  within  the  Project   Implementation
Schedule  and  avoid  sanctions  for  any  delay  thereof.  Also,   projects
delivered promptly serves as beneficial to both  the  municipality  and  its
constituents.

The Municipal Mayor and OIC-Municipal Engineer shall  institute  legal
actions against the contractor for failure to undertake and/or complete  the
project within the periods specified in  the  contract,  including  but  not
limited to:
     a. Termination of the Contract;
     b. Require the contractor to return the mobilization fee  (for  Rehab.
        And Concreting of Moyo-Pase FMR) collected from the  LGU  and  DAR-
        ARCP II;
     c. Blacklist the  contractor  and  disqualify  it  from  participating
        future procurement projects of the LGU; and
     d. Return to DAR-ARCP II any unused project funds.

We recommended that cash advances shall invariably bear the certification of the Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and properly accounted for in the books. We also recommended that the LGU strictly follow provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR, Section 4 and 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on matters of procurement.

We recommended that management effect the transfer of its public infrastructure accounts to the books of accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively; Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs of the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry; Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that Management:

Direct the personnel in-charge of the project and the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA a Status Report on the Implementation of the Project and a satisfactory written explanation as to why liquidations of funds transferred to NGOs/POs remained outstanding in the books of accounts over the last three years;

Demand from the NGOs/POs to return the unutilized fund/excess amount of fund of fund to the LGU; and

Ensure that succeeding fund releases to NGOs/POs strictly adhere to the rules and regulations embodied in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001.

It was recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Analyze transactions to ensure proper recording and classifying of accounts; Effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their appropriate/correct accounts as discussed above; and Ensure that the preparation of the Financial Statements of the LGU comply with the requirements of PPSAS 1, COA Resolution No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and P.D. 1445.

We recommended that:

Management should refrain from charging expenses to POP that partake the nature of confidential transactions, otherwise, the condition will be considered as misappropriation of funds; Disbursements should be supported with complete documentation; and Procurement should be in conformity with RA 9184 (The Government Procurement Reform Act).

We recommended that:

Supplement advertisement of invitation of bids by adopting the policy under Sec. 363 of RA 7160 prescribing sending invitations by mail or otherwise to prospective bidders in the locality or nearby areas in case of high-value procurement to generate offer most advantageous to the government; List of suppliers in the DRL must be considered; Procurement for spare parts specifically for heavy equipment can be made through direct contracting as the brand for the heavy equipment calls for its specific specifications; and Purchase Request for catering services must be supported with menus to determine the type and kind of food offered and the reasonableness of price.

It was recommended that Management:

Submit the necessary documents in the liquidation of the fund transferred; Ensure that in cases of fund transfer to another agency, it must be supported with complete documentation pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the transfer of fund to other agencies as receivable for proper accounting and to determine accountability of the transferred funds.

We recommended that management exercise prudence in the conduct of cultural activities in order to protect and conserve government funds and unnecessary and extravagant expenditures be prevented. We also recommended that payroll for prizes be computer-generated in order to ensure credibility in the data it represents. Erasures on the amounts should be avoided, as these would cast doubt on the reliability of the data.

Require the focal persons to follow up the funding of the submitted project proposals to concern participating national agencies to fast track the release of the BUB funds; and Prioritize the procurement process within the minimum prescribed timeline so as not to delay the distribution and implementation of these BUB projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to stop the grant of RATA to personnel not authorized to receive such benefit as this constitutes irregular disbursements. Instead create a position of Municipal Government Department Head-I / Assistant Department Head-I position to accommodate Mr. Adaza or create a MENRO with funding requirements fully provided. Likewise, the creation must not result to excess in the Personal Services limitations; otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit for being irregular.

We recommended that the Accountant should check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the voucher. Management should provide specific duties and responsibilities of casuals, job orders and contractual employees. Accomplishment reports should likewise be submitted by concerned employees before payment of salaries is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: Supervise the revenue collectors particularly delinquent ones so that there will be regular turn-over of collections to the liquidating officer to deter possible misuse or losses of government funds and hasten the recognition of income. Inform the Municipal Accountant in advance for the amounts to be included in their proper reporting period due to delay in remittance. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and observe proper cut-off for recognition of income to achieve faithful representation of the amounts stated in the financial statements and in order for the municipality to obtain the right to early disposition of its income to support its operations.

We recommended the reparation of the defects noted of the newly constructed vermi house by the contractor Madaja Builders and Construction Supply Corporation under Provision No. 3 and Paragraph 2 of the Contract Agreement and Certificate of Completion. Moreover, it was also recommended that the Municipality for all its project proposals and implementation in the future should provide more suitable or apt project description, characterizing and outlining thereat the nature of the project and its intended purpose, as well as posting tarpaulin signboards in project locations in conformity with Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Further, we recommended the immediate submission of the Project Proposal and Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and Department of Agriculture for our evaluation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the preparation of the Work and Financial Plan containing therein the specific program/projects from the Budget Officer who shall initiate the request for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan of the resolution that would ratify the incurrence of such expenditures. Allocate the 20% EDF to socio-economic development and environmental management projects. Maximize the utilization of 20% EDF allocated for the implementation of various programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the compliance with the requirements on the closure and upgrading of open dump to controlled dump be followed, otherwise, the agency’s plan to convert it to an Ecological Park will not materialize.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from charging expenses that were not within the GAD Plan and Budget. Also, refrain from acquiring goods and supplies through reimbursements since procurement transactions are generally subject to the provisions of R.A. 9184. In addition, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should give emphasis on withholding applicable taxes due from payments made to various suppliers and should be accountable for the accuracy thereof. Finally, we recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan spearheaded by the SB Chairperson on the Committee of Women, Children and Family with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, formulate the GAD Code and its corresponding Manual to guide the LGU and its officials in formulating policies, plans and programs that would effectively address gender-related issues.
